title: Passing Arguments to a Base Constructor
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a two-level hierarchy where the derived class hands an argument up to the base class constructor through its member initializer list. A `Car` *is a* `Vehicle` that also has a name.

## Input

Each line contains a string `name` (single word) and an integer `speed`. Input ends at EOF.

## Output

For every input line, print one line:

```
<name>: <speed> km/h
```

## Constraints

- `Vehicle` stores `speed` privately and has no default constructor; it only offers a constructor taking the speed plus a getter. The derived class therefore cannot set the speed itself -- it must delegate the speed to `Vehicle`'s constructor.
- `Car` adds the name and is responsible for printing each line.

test_cases.label: Zero speed and more
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Parked: 0 km/h
Bike: 45 km/h
Bus: 60 km/h

title: Three-Level Constructor Chain
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a three-level inheritance chain `Creature` -> `Monster` -> `BossMonster` where each level introduces exactly one member and each constructor delegates the data it does not own to its immediate parent. A boss's *effective* strength combines health and armor, so computing it requires reaching members introduced at different levels of the chain.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 to 10), the number of boss monsters.
- Next `N` lines: each has a string `name` (single word), an integer `health`, and an integer `armor`.

## Output

For each boss monster, one line:

```
<name> [<health> hp, <armor> armor] -> <effective> effective
```

where `effective = health + armor`.

## Constraints

- `Creature` owns the name (constructor takes a name; provides a getter). It has no default constructor.
- `Monster` (inherits from `Creature`) adds health, delegating the name upward.
- `BossMonster` (inherits from `Monster`) adds armor, delegating name and health upward, and is responsible for producing the output line. Each class initializes only its own member and passes everything else to its parent.

test_cases.label: Two boss monsters
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Dragon [500 hp, 30 armor] -> 530 effective
Ogre [200 hp, 10 armor] -> 210 effective

test_cases.label: Three bosses
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Golem [1000 hp, 99 armor] -> 1099 effective
Witch [150 hp, 5 armor] -> 155 effective
Demon [666 hp, 42 armor] -> 708 effective
